description: GerryChain ensemble analysis of Georgia redistricting maps

algorithm.proposal: Merge two adjacent districts, re-split via random spanning tree

data_sources.precincts: RDH Georgia 2020 General Election Statewide Precincts (ga_gen_20_st_prec)

methodology.summary: We generate thousands of alternative redistricting maps using a Markov chain random walk (ReCom algorithm). Each map satisfies only legal constraints: equal population, contiguous districts. We then measure where the enacted map falls in the distribution of these neutral alternatives.

metric_definitions.efficiency_gap: Difference in wasted votes (votes for losing candidates + excess winning votes) between parties, expressed as a fraction of total votes.
metric_definitions.mean_median: Difference between a party's mean vote share and median vote share across districts. A large gap suggests the party's voters are spread inefficiently.
metric_definitions.dem_seats: Number of districts where Democrats win a majority of votes.
metric_definitions.polsby_popper: Ratio of district area to the area of a circle with the same perimeter. 1.0 = perfect circle; lower = more irregular shape.
metric_definitions.stability: For each precinct: the fraction of neutral maps where it is assigned to the same district as in the enacted plan. Low stability = contested boundary area.

references: Metric definitions: Stephanopoulos & McGhee (2015), Bernstein & Duchin (2017)
GerryChain: MGGG Redistricting Lab, Tufts University
